content.text:
## Design Section 1: Database Schema

New `release_todos` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE release_todos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CHECK (
        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
    ),
    UNIQUE(release_id),
    UNIQUE(semantic_release_id)
);
```

Key points:
- **1:1 relationship** — each release or semantic release has at most one TODO (enforced by UNIQUE constraints)
- **CHECK constraint** — exactly one of `release_id` or `semantic_release_id` must be set (same pattern as `subscriptions`)
- **CASCADE deletes** — if the underlying release is deleted, the TODO is cleaned up
- **Status transitions:** `pending` → `acknowledged` → `resolved` (one-directional, though we'll allow re-setting to any state via API)

**When are TODOs created?** During notification routing — when the `NotifyWorker` processes a release, it also inserts a `release_todos` row. For semantic releases, the same happens in the semantic release notification path. This keeps it transactional with the existing outbox pattern.

Does this schema look right?

## Design Section 2: API Endpoints

New REST endpoints under `/api/v1/todos`:

```
GET    /api/v1/todos                    — List todos (filterable by status, paginated)
GET    /api/v1/todos/{id}               — Get a single todo
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge   — Mark as acknowledged
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve       — Mark as resolved
```

Query parameters for `GET /api/v1/todos`:
- `?status=pending` / `acknowledged` / `resolved` — filter by status
- `?page=1&per_page=25` — standard pagination

The list endpoint returns todos enriched with release/semantic release metadata (project name, version, provider, repository) via JOINs — so the frontend doesn't need extra round-trips.

**Response shape** (list):
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "status": "pending",
      "release_id": "uuid",        // or null
      "semantic_release_id": "uuid", // or null
      "project_name": "Go Runtime",
      "version": "v1.22.0",
      "provider": "github",
      "repository": "golang/go",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/...",
      "release_url": "/releases/uuid",
      "urgency": "HIGH",           // from semantic report, if applicable
      "created_at": "...",
      "acknowledged_at": null,
      "resolved_at": null
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "total": 42 }
}
```

The acknowledge/resolve endpoints are simple PATCH operations — no request body needed. They set the timestamp and update the status.

**One-click API endpoints** (for notification button links):
```
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true
```

These are GET endpoints (so they work as clickable links from Slack/Discord/email). The `redirect=true` param causes a 302 redirect back to the Todo page in the web portal after updating. This avoids needing POST forms in notification cards.

Does this API design look right?

## Design Section 3: Notification Card Changes

**Slack** — Add two button elements to the existing Block Kit message using `"type": "actions"` block with `"type": "button"` elements. Each button uses a `"url"` field pointing to the one-click GET endpoint. These render as native-looking Slack buttons but open a browser tab. No Slack App required since we're using URL buttons, not interactive callbacks.

```json
{
  "type": "actions",
  "elements": [
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "✅ Acknowledge"}, "url": "https://.../api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true"},
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "☑️ Resolve"}, "url": "https://.../api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true"}
  ]
}
```

**Discord** — Discord webhook embeds don't support interactive buttons without a Bot application. Instead, we'll add the links as markdown in the embed description or as a footer field: `[✅ Acknowledge](url) · [☑️ Resolve](url)`. These render as clickable links in-line.

**Email** — Add two styled HTML buttons (anchor tags styled as buttons) in the email template linking to the one-click endpoints.

**Webhook** — Add `acknowledge_url` and `resolve_url` fields to the JSON payload. Consumers can use these URLs however they want.

**Passing the TODO ID to senders:** The `NotifyWorker` creates the `release_todos` row first, then passes the TODO ID as part of the `Notification` struct (new field: `TodoID string`). Each sender uses it to construct the URLs using the existing `PUBLIC_URL` base.

Does this look right?

## Design Section 4: Frontend — Todo Tab

**New sidebar entry:** "Todo" added to the sidebar navigation between "Dashboard" and "Projects" (high visibility position), using a `CheckSquare` or `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

**Page: `/todo`** — Three sub-tabs for filtering:

| Tab | Filter | Purpose |
|-----|--------|---------|
| **Pending** | `status=pending` | Releases needing attention (default) |
| **Acknowledged** | `status=acknowledged` | Being handled, not yet resolved |
| **Resolved** | `status=resolved` | Completed items |

**Table columns:**
- **Project** — project name
- **Version** — version string (clickable link to release detail)
- **Type** — badge: "Release" or "Semantic" 
- **Provider** — provider badge (GitHub, Docker Hub, etc.)
- **Urgency** — urgency badge (only for semantic releases, blank for source releases)
- **Created** — when the TODO was created (relative time)
- **Actions** — "Acknowledge" / "Resolve" buttons depending on current status

**Behavior:**
- Default view shows "Pending" tab
- Clicking "Acknowledge" on a pending item transitions it to acknowledged (optimistic UI update via SWR mutation)
- Clicking "Resolve" on an acknowledged item transitions it to resolved
- Pending tab shows an "Acknowledge" button; Acknowledged tab shows a "Resolve" button; Resolved tab shows no action buttons
- Count badges on the tab headers showing how many items in each state (e.g., "Pending (3)")
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next) matching existing pages
- SSE integration — new TODO events trigger SWR revalidation so the list stays live

**Styling:** Follows existing patterns — inline styles with CSS variables, DM Sans font, `#e8601a` accent color for primary actions, familiar table layout matching the Releases page.

Does this look right?

## Design Section 5: TODO Creation Flow

**When does a TODO get created?**

A TODO is created at the same point notifications are sent — in the `NotifyWorker`. This ensures:
1. Every notified release gets a TODO (they're paired)
2. It's transactional — if the worker fails, no orphaned TODOs
3. Filtered/excluded releases don't get TODOs (they never reach the worker)

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, after fetching the release and before iterating subscriptions, insert a `release_todos` row with `release_id` set. Use `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` so re-processing a job doesn't create duplicates (the UNIQUE constraint on `release_id` protects us).

**Semantic releases:** The semantic release notification happens in the agent orchestrator after completing a semantic release. At that point, insert a `release_todos` row with `semantic_release_id` set, same `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` pattern.

**Flow diagram:**
```
New release detected
  → INSERT release (ingestion)
  → Enqueue NotifyJobArgs (transactional outbox)
  → NotifyWorker picks up job
    → INSERT release_todos (release_id, status='pending') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
    → Get todo ID (for button URLs)
    → Send notifications with acknowledge/resolve URLs
    → Check agent rules → maybe enqueue AgentJobArgs

Agent completes semantic release
  → INSERT release_todos (semantic_release_id, status='pending') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
  → Get todo ID
  → Send semantic release notifications with URLs
```

**Key detail:** The TODO must be inserted _before_ sending notifications, so the todo ID is available for constructing button URLs.

Does this flow look right?
That covers the full design. Let me write the design doc and commit it.

input.content:
# Design: Release TODO Tracking

## Overview

Add TODO tracking to releases so users can acknowledge and resolve release notifications. Notification cards include action buttons, and a new web portal tab provides a centralized view of pending/acknowledged/resolved items.

## Scope

- Applies to **both** source releases and semantic releases
- **Anonymous** actions (no user tracking)
- Notification buttons: URL-based buttons in Slack/Discord, styled links in email, URLs in webhook payload
- New "Todo" sidebar tab in the web portal

## Database Schema

New `release_todos` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE release_todos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CHECK (
        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
    ),
    UNIQUE(release_id),
    UNIQUE(semantic_release_id)
);
```

- 1:1 with release or semantic release (UNIQUE constraints)
- CHECK constraint ensures exactly one FK is set (same pattern as `subscriptions`)
- CASCADE deletes clean up when underlying release is removed
- Status: `pending` -> `acknowledged` -> `resolved`

## API Endpoints

### CRUD

```
GET    /api/v1/todos                    -- List (filterable: ?status=pending|acknowledged|resolved, paginated)
GET    /api/v1/todos/{id}              -- Get single todo
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge  -- Mark acknowledged
PATCH  /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve      -- Mark resolved
```

### One-Click (for notification buttons)

```
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true  -- Acknowledge + 302 redirect to web portal
GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true       -- Resolve + 302 redirect to web portal
```

GET endpoints so they work as clickable links from Slack/Discord/email. The `redirect=true` param triggers a 302 redirect to the Todo page after updating.

### List Response Shape

```json
{
  "data": [{
    "id": "uuid",
    "status": "pending",
    "release_id": "uuid",
    "semantic_release_id": null,
    "project_name": "Go Runtime",
    "version": "v1.22.0",
    "provider": "github",
    "repository": "golang/go",
    "source_url": "https://github.com/golang/go/releases/tag/v1.22.0",
    "release_url": "/releases/uuid",
    "urgency": null,
    "created_at": "...",
    "acknowledged_at": null,
    "resolved_at": null
  }],
  "meta": { "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "total": 42 }
}
```

List endpoint returns enriched data via JOINs (project name, version, provider, etc.) to avoid extra round-trips.

## Notification Card Changes

### Slack

Add `"type": "actions"` block with URL buttons:

```json
{
  "type": "actions",
  "elements": [
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Acknowledge"}, "url": "{PUBLIC_URL}/api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge?redirect=true"},
    {"type": "button", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Resolve"}, "url": "{PUBLIC_URL}/api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve?redirect=true"}
  ]
}
```

URL buttons look native in Slack but open a browser. No Slack App required.

### Discord

Add markdown links in embed description (Discord webhooks don't support buttons without a Bot):

```
[Acknowledge](url) | [Resolve](url)
```

### Email

Add styled HTML anchor tags (button-styled) in the email template.

### Webhook

Add `acknowledge_url` and `resolve_url` fields to JSON payload.

### Passing TODO ID

`Notification` struct gets a new `TodoID string` field. `NotifyWorker` creates the TODO row first, then passes the ID to senders for URL construction.

## TODO Creation Flow

TODOs are created during notification routing:

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, before sending notifications:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

**Semantic releases:** In the agent orchestrator's notification path, after completing a semantic release:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (semantic_release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ensures idempotency (safe for job retries).

## Frontend: Todo Tab

### Sidebar

New "Todo" entry between Dashboard and Projects, using `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

### Page: `/todo`

Three sub-tabs with count badges:

| Tab | Filter | Default |
|-----|--------|---------|
| Pending (N) | `status=pending` | Yes |
| Acknowledged (N) | `status=acknowledged` | |
| Resolved (N) | `status=resolved` | |

### Table Columns

| Column | Content |
|--------|---------|
| Project | Project name |
| Version | Version string (links to release detail) |
| Type | Badge: "Release" or "Semantic" |
| Provider | Provider badge |
| Urgency | Urgency badge (semantic only) |
| Created | Relative timestamp |
| Actions | Acknowledge/Resolve buttons |

### Behavior

- Pending tab: "Acknowledge" button per row
- Acknowledged tab: "Resolve" button per row
- Resolved tab: no action buttons
- Optimistic UI updates via SWR mutation
- SSE revalidation for live updates
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next)
- Follows existing styling patterns (inline styles, CSS variables, DM Sans)

`Notification` struct gets a new `TodoID string` field. `NotifyWorker` creates the TODO row first, then passes the ID to senders for URL construction.

## TODO Creation Flow

TODOs are created during notification routing:

**Source releases:** In `NotifyWorker.Work()`, before sending notifications:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

**Semantic releases:** In the agent orchestrator's notification path, after completing a semantic release:
1. `INSERT INTO release_todos (semantic_release_id) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`
2. Get the todo ID
3. Pass to senders via `Notification.TodoID`

`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ensures idempotency (safe for job retries).

## Frontend: Todo Tab

### Sidebar

New "Todo" entry between Dashboard and Projects, using `ListTodo` icon from lucide-react.

### Page: `/todo`

Three sub-tabs with count badges:

| Tab | Filter | Default |
|-----|--------|---------|
| Pending (N) | `status=pending` | Yes |
| Acknowledged (N) | `status=acknowledged` | |
| Resolved (N) | `status=resolved` | |

### Table Columns

| Column | Content |
|--------|---------|
| Project | Project name |
| Version | Version string (links to release detail) |
| Type | Badge: "Release" or "Semantic" |
| Provider | Provider badge |
| Urgency | Urgency badge (semantic only) |
| Created | Relative timestamp |
| Actions | Acknowledge/Resolve buttons |

### Behavior

- Pending tab: "Acknowledge" button per row
- Acknowledged tab: "Resolve" button per row
- Resolved tab: no action buttons
- Optimistic UI updates via SWR mutation
- SSE revalidation for live updates
- Standard pagination (Previous/Next)
- Follows existing styling patterns (inline styles, CSS variables, DM Sans)

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

**Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (this session)** - I dispatch fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Parallel Session (separate)** - Open new session with executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Stay in this session
- Fresh subagent per task + code review

**If Parallel Session chosen:**
- Guide them to open new session in worktree
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** New session uses superpowers:executing-plans

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me first look at the key files I need to reference for exact line numbers and patterns.

8. `web/lib/api/client.ts` — The API client pattern for adding a new resource

- The patterns used (function signatures, struct definitions, etc.)

Be thorough — this is for writing a detailed implementation plan.

content.content:
1→package db
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     9→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/rivermigrate"
    10→)
    11→
    12→const schema = `
    13→-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
    14→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    15→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    16→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    17→    description TEXT,
    18→    agent_prompt TEXT,
    19→    agent_rules JSONB,
    20→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    21→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
    25→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sources (
    26→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    29→    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    30→    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400,
    31→    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    32→    config JSONB,
    33→    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34→    last_error TEXT,
    35→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    36→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    37→    UNIQUE(provider, repository)
    38→);
    39→
    40→-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
    41→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS context_sources (
    42→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    43→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    45→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    46→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    47→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    48→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    49→);
    50→
    51→-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
    52→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS releases (
    53→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    54→    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    55→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    56→    raw_data JSONB,
    57→    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    58→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    59→    UNIQUE(source_id, version)
    60→);
    61→
    62→-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
    63→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_releases (
    64→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    67→    report JSONB,
    68→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    69→    error TEXT,
    70→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    71→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    72→    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
    73→);
    74→
    75→-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
    76→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_release_sources (
    77→    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    78→    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    79→    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
    80→);
    81→
    82→-- Notification channels (standalone)
    83→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (
    84→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    85→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    86→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    87→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    88→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    89→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    90→);
    91→
    92→-- Subscriptions: two types (source releases and semantic releases)
    93→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    94→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    95→    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    96→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release')),
    97→    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98→    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→    version_filter TEXT,
   100→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   101→    CHECK (
   102→        (type = 'source_release'   AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   103→        (type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   104→    )
   105→);
   106→
   107→-- Agent runs (scoped to project)
   108→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs (
   109→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111→    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   112→    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   113→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   114→    prompt_used TEXT,
   115→    error TEXT,
   116→    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   117→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   118→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   119→);
   120→
   121→ALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);
   122→
   123→-- API authentication keys
   124→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_keys (
   125→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   126→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   127→    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   128→    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,
   129→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   130→    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   131→);
   132→
   133→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on new releases
   134→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_release_created() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   135→BEGIN
   136→    PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   137→    RETURN NEW;
   138→END;
   139→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   140→
   141→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS release_created_trigger ON releases;
   142→CREATE TRIGGER release_created_trigger
   143→    AFTER INSERT ON releases
   144→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_release_created();
   145→
   146→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on semantic release completion
   147→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   148→BEGIN
   149→    IF NEW.status = 'completed' AND (OLD IS NULL OR OLD.status != 'completed') THEN
   150→        PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'semantic_release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   151→    END IF;
   152→    RETURN NEW;
   153→END;
   154→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   155→
   156→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS semantic_release_trigger ON semantic_releases;
   157→CREATE TRIGGER semantic_release_trigger
   158→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON semantic_releases
   159→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release();
   160→`
   161→
   162→// RunMigrations applies River's schema and the application schema. Idempotent — safe to call on every startup.
   163→func RunMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   164→	migrator, err := rivermigrate.New(riverpgxv5.New(pool), nil)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		return fmt.Errorf("create river migrator: %w", err)
   167→	}
   168→	if _, err := migrator.Migrate(ctx, rivermigrate.DirectionUp, nil); err != nil {
   169→		return fmt.Errorf("river migrations: %w", err)
   170→	}
   171→
   172→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, schema); err != nil {
   173→		return fmt.Errorf("app migrations: %w", err)
   174→	}
   175→
   176→	// Migrate existing subscription type values: source→source_release, project→semantic_release.
   177→	// Drop old CHECK constraints first (both the named type check and the unnamed
   178→	// composite check referencing old values), then UPDATE data, then re-add constraints.
   179→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   180→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_type_check;
   181→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_check;
   182→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'source_release' WHERE type = 'source';
   183→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'semantic_release' WHERE type = 'project';
   184→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_type_check CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release'));
   185→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_check CHECK (
   186→			(type = 'source_release'   AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   187→			(type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL)
   188→		);
   189→	`); err != nil {
   190→		return fmt.Errorf("subscription type migration: %w", err)
   191→	}
   192→
   193→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   194→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_include TEXT;
   195→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_exclude TEXT;
   196→	`); err != nil {
   197→		return fmt.Errorf("source version filter migration: %w", err)
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   201→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS exclude_prereleases BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
   202→	`); err != nil {
   203→		return fmt.Errorf("source exclude_prereleases migration: %w", err)
   204→	}
   205→	return nil
   206→}
   207→

content.content:
1→package routing
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"net/url"
     8→	"regexp"
     9→	"strings"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// Notification is the payload sent through all notification channels.
    15→type Notification struct {
    16→	Title       string `json:"title"`
    17→	Body        string `json:"body"`
    18→	Version     string `json:"version"`
    19→	ProjectName string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    20→	Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    21→	Repository  string `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    22→	ReleaseURL  string `json:"release_url,omitempty"`  // internal Changelogue link
    23→	SourceURL   string `json:"source_url,omitempty"`   // upstream provider link
    24→}
    25→
    26→// Sender is the interface that all notification channel implementations must satisfy.
    27→type Sender interface {
    28→	Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error
    29→}
    30→
    31→// rawBodyFields contains the human-readable fields extracted from raw release JSON.
    32→type rawBodyFields struct {
    33→	Changelog  string
    34→	ReleaseURL string
    35→}
    36→
    37→// parseRawBody extracts known fields from a raw JSON release body.
    38→func parseRawBody(body string) (rawBodyFields, bool) {
    39→	var raw map[string]interface{}
    40→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(body), &raw); err != nil {
    41→		return rawBodyFields{}, false
    42→	}
    43→
    44→	fields := rawBodyFields{
    45→		Changelog:  stringField(raw, "changelog"),
    46→		ReleaseURL: stringField(raw, "release_url"),
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	if fields.Changelog == "" && fields.ReleaseURL == "" {
    50→		return fields, false
    51→	}
    52→	return fields, true
    53→}
    54→
    55→func stringField(m map[string]interface{}, key string) string {
    56→	v, _ := m[key].(string)
    57→	return v
    58→}
    59→
    60→// ProviderURL builds the upstream release page URL for a given provider.
    61→func ProviderURL(provider, repository, version string) string {
    62→	switch strings.ToLower(provider) {
    63→	case "github":
    64→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://github.com/%s/releases/tag/%s", repository, version)
    65→	case "dockerhub":
    66→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/r/%s/tags?name=%s", repository, url.QueryEscape(version))
    67→	case "ecr-public":
    68→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://gallery.ecr.aws/%s", repository)
    69→	case "gitlab":
    70→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://gitlab.com/%s/-/releases/%s", repository, version)
    71→	default:
    72→		return ""
    73→	}
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ProviderLabel returns a human-readable label for a provider.
    77→func ProviderLabel(provider string) string {
    78→	switch strings.ToLower(provider) {
    79→	case "github":
    80→		return "GitHub"
    81→	case "dockerhub":
    82→		return "Docker Hub"
    83→	case "ecr-public":
    84→		return "ECR Public"
    85→	case "gitlab":
    86→		return "GitLab"
    87→	default:
    88→		return provider
    89→	}
    90→}
    91→
    92→// Regex patterns for markdown-to-ASCII conversion.
    93→var (
    94→	// GitHub PR/issue URLs → #NNN ( url )
    95→	reGitHubPR = regexp.MustCompile(`https?://github\.com/([^/]+/[^/]+)/(pull|issues)/(\d+)`)
    96→	// Markdown links [text](url) → text ( url )
    97→	reMdLink = regexp.MustCompile(`\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)`)
    98→	// Headings ## Text → dashed ASCII box
    99→	reHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$`)
   100→	// Bold **text** and __text__
   101→	reBoldStar = regexp.MustCompile(`\*\*(.+?)\*\*`)
   102→	reBoldUnderscore = regexp.MustCompile(`__(.+?)__`)
   103→	// Inline code `text`
   104→	reInlineCode = regexp.MustCompile("`([^`]+)`")
   105→	// HTML tags
   106→	reHTML = regexp.MustCompile(`<[^>]+>`)
   107→	// Images ![alt](url)
   108→	reImage = regexp.MustCompile(`!\[([^\]]*)\]\([^)]+\)`)
   109→)
   110→
   111→// markdownToASCII converts GitHub-flavored Markdown into clean ASCII text
   112→// suitable for display inside Slack/Discord code blocks.
   113→func markdownToASCII(md string) string {
   114→	s := md
   115→
   116→	// Strip images first (before link processing)
   117→	s = reImage.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   118→
   119→	// Convert GitHub PR/issue URLs to #NNN ( url )
   120→	s = reGitHubPR.ReplaceAllString(s, "#$3 ( $0 )")
   121→
   122→	// Convert markdown links [text](url) → text ( url )
   123→	s = reMdLink.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1 ( $2 )")
   124→
   125→	// Convert headings to ASCII art with dashes
   126→	s = reHeading.ReplaceAllStringFunc(s, func(match string) string {
   127→		parts := reHeading.FindStringSubmatch(match)
   128→		title := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
   129→		dashes := strings.Repeat("-", len(title))
   130→		return dashes + "\n" + title + "\n" + dashes
   131→	})
   132→
   133→	// Strip bold markers
   134→	s = reBoldStar.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   135→	s = reBoldUnderscore.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   136→
   137→	// Strip inline code backticks
   138→	s = reInlineCode.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   139→
   140→	// Strip HTML tags
   141→	s = reHTML.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
   142→
   143→	// Collapse 3+ consecutive blank lines into 2
   144→	reBlankLines := regexp.MustCompile(`\n{3,}`)
   145→	s = reBlankLines.ReplaceAllString(s, "\n\n")
   146→
   147→	return strings.TrimSpace(s)
   148→}
   149→

1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"net/http"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9→)
    10→
    11→// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12→type Dependencies struct {
    13→	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14→	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15→	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16→	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17→	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18→	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19→	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20→	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21→	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22→	KeyStore              KeyStore
    23→	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    24→	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    25→	NoAuth                bool
    26→	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    27→	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    28→}
    29→
    30→// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    31→func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    32→	// Public chain: no auth required.
    33→	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    34→
    35→	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    36→	chain := publicChain
    37→	if !deps.NoAuth {
    38→		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	// Projects (CRUD)
    42→	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    43→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    44→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    45→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    46→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    47→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    48→
    49→	// Sources (nested under projects)
    50→	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    51→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    52→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    53→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    54→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    55→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    56→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    57→
    58→	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    59→	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    60→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    61→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    62→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    63→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    64→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    65→
    66→	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    67→	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    68→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    69→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    70→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    71→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    72→
    73→	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    74→	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    75→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    76→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    77→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    78→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    79→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    80→
    81→	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    82→	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    83→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    84→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    85→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    86→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    87→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    88→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    89→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    90→
    91→	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    92→	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
    93→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    94→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    95→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    96→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
    97→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
    98→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
    99→
   100→	// Agent
   101→	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   102→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   103→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   104→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   105→
   106→	// Providers (metadata — static, no store needed)
   107→	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   108→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   109→
   110→	// Discovery (public — no auth, proxies external APIs)
   111→	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   112→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   113→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   114→
   115→	// SSE events
   116→	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   117→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   118→
   119→	// Health (public — no auth middleware)
   120→	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   121→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   122→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   123→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   124→}
   125→

    17→// PgStore implements all store interfaces using a PostgreSQL connection pool.
    18→type PgStore struct {
    19→	pool  *pgxpool.Pool
    20→	river *river.Client[pgx.Tx]
    21→}
    22→
    23→// NewPgStore returns a new PgStore backed by the given connection pool and River client.
    24→// The river client may be nil if agent triggering is not needed.
    25→func NewPgStore(pool *pgxpool.Pool, riverClient *river.Client[pgx.Tx]) *PgStore {

339→func (s *PgStore) ListReleasesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int, includeExcluded bool) ([]models.Release, int, error) {
   340→	var total int
   341→	if includeExcluded {
   342→		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   343→			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM releases r JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id WHERE s.project_id = $1`,
   344→			projectID).Scan(&total)
   345→		if err != nil {
   346→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count releases: %w", err)
   347→		}
   348→	} else {
   349→		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   350→			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM releases r JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id WHERE s.project_id = $1
   351→			   AND (s.version_filter_include IS NULL OR r.version ~ s.version_filter_include)
   352→			   AND (s.version_filter_exclude IS NULL OR r.version !~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   353→			   AND (s.exclude_prereleases = false OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' IS NULL OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' != 'true')`,
   354→			projectID).Scan(&total)
   355→		if err != nil {
   356→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count releases: %w", err)
   357→		}
   358→	}
   359→	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
   360→	var rows pgx.Rows
   361→	var err error
   362→	if includeExcluded {
   363→		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
   364→			`SELECT r.id, r.source_id, r.version, COALESCE(r.raw_data,'{}'), r.released_at, r.created_at,
   365→			        p.id, p.name, s.provider, s.repository,
   366→			        CASE WHEN
   367→			          (s.version_filter_include IS NOT NULL AND r.version !~ s.version_filter_include)
   368→			          OR (s.version_filter_exclude IS NOT NULL AND r.version ~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   369→			          OR (s.exclude_prereleases = true AND r.raw_data->>'prerelease' = 'true')
   370→			        THEN true ELSE false END
   371→			 FROM releases r
   372→			 JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   373→			 JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.id
   374→			 WHERE s.project_id = $1
   375→			 ORDER BY COALESCE(r.released_at, r.created_at) DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3`, projectID, perPage, offset)
   376→	} else {
   377→		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
   378→			`SELECT r.id, r.source_id, r.version, COALESCE(r.raw_data,'{}'), r.released_at, r.created_at,
   379→			        p.id, p.name, s.provider, s.repository,
   380→			        false
   381→			 FROM releases r
   382→			 JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   383→			 JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.id
   384→			 WHERE s.project_id = $1
   385→			   AND (s.version_filter_include IS NULL OR r.version ~ s.version_filter_include)
   386→			   AND (s.version_filter_exclude IS NULL OR r.version !~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   387→			   AND (s.exclude_prereleases = false OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' IS NULL OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' != 'true')
   388→			 ORDER BY COALESCE(r.released_at, r.created_at) DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3`, projectID, perPage, offset)
   389→	}
   390→	if err != nil {
   391→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list releases by project: %w", err)
   392→	}
   393→	defer rows.Close()
   394→	var releases []models.Release
   395→	for rows.Next() {
   396→		var rel models.Release
   397→		if err := rows.Scan(&rel.ID, &rel.SourceID, &rel.Version, &rel.RawData, &rel.ReleasedAt, &rel.CreatedAt,
   398→			&rel.ProjectID, &rel.ProjectName, &rel.Provider, &rel.Repository, &rel.Excluded); err != nil {
   399→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("scan release: %w", err)
   400→		}
   401→		releases = append(releases, rel)
   402→	}
   403→	return releases, total, nil
   404→}
   405→
   406→func (s *PgStore) GetRelease(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Release, error) {
   407→	var rel models.Release
   408→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   409→		`SELECT r.id, r.source_id, r.version, COALESCE(r.raw_data,'{}'), r.released_at, r.created_at,
   410→		        COALESCE(p.id::text,''), COALESCE(p.name,''), COALESCE(s.provider,''), COALESCE(s.repository,'')
   411→		 FROM releases r
   412→		 LEFT JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   413→		 LEFT JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.id
   414→		 WHERE r.id = $1`, id,
   415→	).Scan(&rel.ID, &rel.SourceID, &rel.Version, &rel.RawData, &rel.ReleasedAt, &rel.CreatedAt,
   416→		&rel.ProjectID, &rel.ProjectName, &rel.Provider, &rel.Repository)
   417→	if err != nil {
   418→		return nil, err
   419→	}
   420→	return &rel, nil
   421→}
   422→
   423→// --- SubscriptionsStore ---
   424→
   425→func (s *PgStore) ListSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, page, perPage int) ([]models.Subscription, int, error) {
   426→	var total int
   427→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions`).Scan(&total)
   428→	if err != nil {
   429→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count subscriptions: %w", err)
   430→	}
   431→	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
   432→	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
   433→		`SELECT id, channel_id, type, source_id, project_id, COALESCE(version_filter,''), created_at
   434→		 FROM subscriptions ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, perPage, offset)
   435→	if err != nil {
   436→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list subscriptions: %w", err)
   437→	}
   438→	defer rows.Close()

     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→type Release struct {
     9→	ID         string          `json:"id"`
    10→	SourceID   string          `json:"source_id"`
    11→	Version    string          `json:"version"`
    12→	RawData    json.RawMessage `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    13→	ReleasedAt *time.Time      `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    14→	CreatedAt  time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    15→
    16→	// Enriched fields from JOINed sources/projects tables.
    17→	ProjectID   string `json:"project_id,omitempty"`
    18→	ProjectName string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    19→	Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    20→	Repository  string `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    21→	Excluded    bool   `json:"excluded"` // true when filtered out by source version filters
    22→}
    23→

content.content:
1→package routing
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bytes"
     5→	"context"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    11→)
    12→
    13→type webhookConfig struct {
    14→	URL string `json:"url"`
    15→}
    16→
    17→// WebhookSender sends notifications as JSON POST requests to a configured URL.
    18→type WebhookSender struct {
    19→	Client *http.Client
    20→}
    21→
    22→// webhookSemanticPayload is the structured webhook payload for semantic reports.
    23→type webhookSemanticPayload struct {
    24→	Title       string                `json:"title"`
    25→	Version     string                `json:"version"`
    26→	ProjectName string                `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    27→	Provider    string                `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    28→	Repository  string                `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    29→	ReleaseURL  string                `json:"release_url,omitempty"`
    30→	SourceURL   string                `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
    31→	Report      *models.SemanticReport `json:"report"`
    32→}
    33→
    34→func (s *WebhookSender) Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error {
    35→	var cfg webhookConfig
    36→	if err := json.Unmarshal(ch.Config, &cfg); err != nil {
    37→		return fmt.Errorf("parse webhook config: %w", err)
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	// Try to parse body as SemanticReport for structured output.
    41→	var payload any
    42→	var report models.SemanticReport
    43→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(msg.Body), &report); err == nil && report.Subject != "" {
    44→		payload = webhookSemanticPayload{
    45→			Title:       msg.Title,
    46→			Version:     msg.Version,
    47→			ProjectName: msg.ProjectName,
    48→			Provider:    msg.Provider,
    49→			Repository:  msg.Repository,
    50→			ReleaseURL:  msg.ReleaseURL,
    51→			SourceURL:   msg.SourceURL,
    52→			Report:      &report,
    53→		}
    54→	} else {
    55→		payload = msg
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    59→	if err != nil {
    60→		return fmt.Errorf("marshal notification: %w", err)
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, cfg.URL, bytes.NewReader(body))
    64→	if err != nil {
    65→		return fmt.Errorf("create request: %w", err)
    66→	}
    67→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    68→
    69→	client := s.Client
    70→	if client == nil {
    71→		client = http.DefaultClient
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	resp, err := client.Do(req)
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		return fmt.Errorf("send webhook: %w", err)
    77→	}
    78→	defer resp.Body.Close()
    79→
    80→	if resp.StatusCode >= 400 {
    81→		return fmt.Errorf("webhook returned status %d", resp.StatusCode)
    82→	}
    83→	return nil
    84→}
    85→

content.content:
1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"google.golang.org/genai"
    13→
    14→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    15→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent/llmagent"
    16→	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    17→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin"
    18→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin/loggingplugin"
    19→	"google.golang.org/adk/runner"
    20→	"google.golang.org/adk/session"
    21→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    22→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/geminitool"
    23→
    24→	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    25→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    26→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    27→)
    28→
    29→// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    30→// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    31→// the resulting semantic release.
    32→type OrchestratorStore interface {
    33→	AgentDataStore
    34→	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    35→	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    36→	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    37→	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    38→	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    39→	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    40→	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    41→	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    42→}
    43→
    44→const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    45→
    46→Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    47→
    48→Use the available tools to:
    49→1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    50→2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    51→3. Check binary/image availability.
    52→4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    53→5. Always search the web for supplementary context about this release
    54→   (community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues, migration guides, direct download links).
    55→
    56→CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    57→Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.
    58→
    59→For download_links: ONLY include URLs where the user can actually download the software — direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) and the GitHub/project release page. Do NOT include CVE links, security advisory URLs, blog posts, documentation, or any non-download URLs.
    60→
    61→The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    62→{
    63→  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    64→  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    65→  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    66→  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    67→  "changelog_summary": "summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    68→  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    69→  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15", "curl -L -o geth.tar.gz https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz"],
    70→  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    71→}`
    72→
    73→// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    74→// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    75→// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    76→func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    77→	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    78→	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    79→		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    80→	}
    81→	if version != "" {
    82→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    83→	} else {
    84→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
    85→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    94→	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    95→	if err != nil {
    96→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   100→	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   101→		Name:        "data_agent",
   102→		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   103→		Model:       llmModel,
   104→		Tools:       functionTools,
   105→	})
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   111→	var searchTool tool.Tool
   112→	var searchModel model.LLM
   113→	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   114→	case "openai":
   115→		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   116→		searchTool = *wsConfig
   117→		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   118→		if err != nil {
   119→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   120→		}
   121→	default: // gemini
   122→		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   123→		searchModel = llmModel
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	// Fetch project sources to give the search agent context about where releases come from.
   127→	sources, _, err := store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, project.ID, 1, 100)
   128→	if err != nil {
   129→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list project sources: %w", err)
   130→	}
   131→	var sourceLines []string
   132→	for _, s := range sources {
   133→		sourceLines = append(sourceLines, fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s", s.Provider, s.Repository))
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	searchInstruction := fmt.Sprintf(
   137→		"You are a web search assistant for the project %q. Always include the project name in your search queries to get relevant results. Focus on this specific project — ignore results for unrelated projects that happen to share the same version number.",
   138→		project.Name,
   139→	)
   140→	if len(sourceLines) > 0 {
   141→		searchInstruction += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nThis project's upstream sources are:\n%s\nUse these repository names and URLs to refine your search queries.", strings.Join(sourceLines, "\n"))
   142→	}
   143→
   144→	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   145→		Name:        "search_agent",
   146→		Description: "Search the web for context about a release — community sentiment, security advisories, adoption statistics, known issues, and migration guides. Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights.",
   147→		Instruction: searchInstruction,
   148→		Model:       searchModel,
   149→		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   150→	})
   151→	if err != nil {
   152→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   156→	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   157→		Name:        "release_analyst",
   158→		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   159→		Model:       llmModel,
   160→		Instruction: instruction,
   161→		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   162→			newTracedAgentTool(dataAgent),
   163→			newTracedAgentTool(searchAgent),
   164→		},
   165→	})
   166→}
   167→
   168→// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   169→// release matching the target version.
   170→func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   171→	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   172→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   175→	}
   176→	if len(sources) == 0 {
   177→		return true, nil
   178→	}
   179→
   180→	for _, src := range sources {
   181→		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   182→		if err != nil {
   183→			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   184→		}
   185→		if !has {
   186→			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   187→				"project_id", projectID,
   188→				"source_id", src.ID,
   189→				"version", version,
   190→			)
   191→			return false, nil
   192→		}
   193→	}
   194→	return true, nil
   195→}
   196→
   197→// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   198→// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   199→// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   200→type Orchestrator struct {

content.content:
1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  BatchSubscriptionInput,
    12→  BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput,
    13→  NotificationChannel,
    14→  ChannelInput,
    15→  ContextSource,
    16→  ContextSourceInput,
    17→  SemanticRelease,
    18→  AgentRun,
    19→  HealthStatus,
    20→  Stats,
    21→  TrendData,
    22→  DiscoverItem,
    23→} from "./types";
    24→
    25→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    26→
    27→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    28→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    29→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    30→    ...init,
    31→  });
    32→  if (!res.ok) {
    33→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    34→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    35→  }
    36→  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    37→  return res.json();
    38→}
    39→
    40→// --- Projects ---
    41→
    42→export const projects = {
    43→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    44→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    45→  get: (id: string) =>
    46→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    47→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    48→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    49→      method: "POST",
    50→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    51→    }),
    52→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    53→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    54→      method: "PUT",
    55→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    56→    }),
    57→  delete: (id: string) =>
    58→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    59→};
    60→
    61→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    62→
    63→export const sources = {
    64→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    65→    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    66→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    67→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    68→      method: "POST",
    69→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    70→    }),
    71→  get: (id: string) =>
    72→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    73→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    74→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    75→      method: "PUT",
    76→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    77→    }),
    78→  delete: (id: string) =>
    79→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    80→  poll: (id: string) =>
    81→    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {
    82→      method: "POST",
    83→    }),
    84→};
    85→
    86→export const releases = {
    87→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    88→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    89→      `/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    90→    ),
    91→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
    92→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
    93→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    94→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    95→      `/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    96→    ),
    97→  get: (id: string) =>
    98→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
    99→};
   100→
   101→// --- Context Sources ---
   102→
   103→export const contextSources = {
   104→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   105→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources?page=${page}`),
   106→  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   107→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources`, {
   108→      method: "POST",
   109→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   110→    }),
   111→  get: (id: string) =>
   112→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`),
   113→  update: (id: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   114→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, {
   115→      method: "PUT",
   116→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   117→    }),
   118→  delete: (id: string) =>
   119→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   120→};
   121→
   122→// --- Semantic Releases ---
   123→
   124→export const semanticReleases = {
   125→  listAll: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   126→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   127→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   128→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   129→  get: (id: string) =>
   130→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`),
   131→  delete: (id: string) =>
   132→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   133→  getSources: (id: string) =>
   134→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}/sources`),
   135→};
   136→
   137→// --- Agent ---
   138→
   139→export const agent = {
   140→  triggerRun: (projectId: string, version?: string) =>
   141→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/run`, {
   142→      method: "POST",
   143→      body: JSON.stringify({ trigger: "test", version: version || undefined }),
   144→    }),
   145→  listRuns: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   146→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/runs?page=${page}`),
   147→  getRun: (id: string) =>
   148→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/agent-runs/${id}`),
   149→};
   150→
   151→// --- Subscriptions ---
   152→
   153→export const subscriptions = {
   154→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   155→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>(`/subscriptions?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   156→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`),
   157→  create: (input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   158→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>("/subscriptions", {
   159→      method: "POST",
   160→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   161→    }),
   162→  batchCreate: (input: BatchSubscriptionInput) =>
   163→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   164→      method: "POST",
   165→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   166→    }),
   167→  update: (id: string, input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   168→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, {
   169→      method: "PUT",
   170→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   171→    }),
   172→  delete: (id: string) =>
   173→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   174→  batchDelete: (input: BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput) =>
   175→    request<ApiResponse<null>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   176→      method: "DELETE",
   177→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   178→    }),
   179→};
   180→
   181→// --- Notification Channels ---
   182→
   183→export const channels = {
   184→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   185→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel[]>>(`/channels?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   186→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`),
   187→  create: (input: ChannelInput) =>
   188→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>("/channels", {
   189→      method: "POST",
   190→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   191→    }),
   192→  update: (id: string, input: ChannelInput) =>
   193→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`, {
   194→      method: "PUT",
   195→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   196→    }),
   197→  delete: (id: string) =>
   198→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/channels/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   199→  test: (id: string) =>
   200→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/channels/${id}/test`, {
   201→      method: "POST",
   202→    }),
   203→};
   204→
   205→// --- System ---
   206→
   207→export const system = {
   208→  health: () => request<HealthStatus>("/health"),
   209→  stats: () => request<ApiResponse<Stats>>("/stats"),
   210→  trend: (granularity: "daily" | "weekly" | "monthly" = "daily", days = 7) =>
   211→    request<ApiResponse<TrendData>>(`/stats/trend?granularity=${granularity}&days=${days}`),
   212→};
   213→
   214→// --- Discovery ---
   215→
   216→export const discover = {
   217→  github: (params?: { q?: string; language?: string }) => {
   218→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   219→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   220→    if (params?.language) search.set("language", params.language);
   221→    const qs = search.toString();
   222→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/github${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   223→  },
   224→  dockerhub: (params?: { q?: string }) => {
   225→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   226→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   227→    const qs = search.toString();
   228→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/dockerhub${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   229→  },
   230→};

1→package routing
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bytes"
     5→	"context"
     6→	"crypto/tls"
     7→	_ "embed"
     8→	"encoding/json"
     9→	"fmt"
    10→	"html/template"
    11→	"log/slog"
    12→	"mime/multipart"
    13→	"net"
    14→	"net/smtp"
    15→	"net/textproto"
    16→	"strings"
    17→	"time"
    18→
    19→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    20→)
    21→
    22→//go:embed email.html.tmpl
    23→var emailTemplateRaw string
    24→
    25→var emailTmpl = template.Must(template.New("email").Parse(emailTemplateRaw))
    26→
    27→type emailConfig struct {
    28→	SMTPHost    string   `json:"smtp_host"`
    29→	SMTPPort    int      `json:"smtp_port"`
    30→	Username    string   `json:"username"`
    31→	Password    string   `json:"password"`
    32→	FromAddress string   `json:"from_address"`
    33→	ToAddresses []string `json:"to_addresses"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→// emailData is the template rendering context.
    37→type emailData struct {
    38→	Subject         string
    39→	UrgencyBadge    string
    40→	UrgencyColor    string
    41→	UrgencyReason   string
    42→	Summary         string
    43→	Changelog       string
    44→	DownloadCommand string
    45→	SourceURL       string
    46→	ReleaseURL      string
    47→	ProviderLabel   string
    48→	FooterText      string
    49→}
    50→
    51→// EmailSender sends notifications via SMTP email.
    52→type EmailSender struct{}
    53→
    54→// sanitizeHeader strips CR and LF characters to prevent SMTP header injection.
    55→func sanitizeHeader(s string) string {
    56→	return strings.NewReplacer("\r", "", "\n", "").Replace(s)
    57→}
    58→
    59→func urgencyColor(level string) string {
    60→	switch strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(level)) {
    61→	case "CRITICAL":
    62→		return "#111113"
    63→	case "HIGH":
    64→		return "#DC2626"
    65→	case "MEDIUM":
    66→		return "#D97706"
    67→	case "LOW":
    68→		return "#16A34A"
    69→	default:
    70→		return "#2563EB"
    71→	}
    72→}
    73→
    74→func (s *EmailSender) Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error {
    75→	var cfg emailConfig
    76→	if err := json.Unmarshal(ch.Config, &cfg); err != nil {
    77→		return fmt.Errorf("parse email config: %w", err)
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	if len(cfg.ToAddresses) == 0 {
    81→		return fmt.Errorf("email config: to_addresses is empty")
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	data := s.buildEmailData(msg)
    85→
    86→	// Render HTML body.
    87→	var htmlBuf bytes.Buffer
    88→	if err := emailTmpl.Execute(&htmlBuf, data); err != nil {
    89→		return fmt.Errorf("render email template: %w", err)
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	// Build plain text body.
    93→	plainText := s.buildPlainText(data)
    94→
    95→	// Assemble multipart/alternative MIME message.
    96→	var msgBuf bytes.Buffer
    97→	msgBuf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("From: %s\r\n", sanitizeHeader(cfg.FromAddress)))
    98→	msgBuf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("To: %s\r\n", sanitizeHeader(strings.Join(cfg.ToAddresses, ", "))))
    99→	msgBuf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Subject: %s\r\n", sanitizeHeader(data.Subject)))
   100→	msgBuf.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n")

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## Implementation Plan Reference: Key Patterns, Line Numbers & Structures

I've thoroughly analyzed all the requested files. Here's the complete reference guide organized by component:

### 1. Database Layer: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go`

**Last CREATE TABLE Statement:**
- **Line 108-119**: `agent_runs` table (final core table before the ALTER/TRIGGER section)
- **Where to add new table**: After line 119 (after `ALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);`) and before line 123

**Pattern for new table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS todos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    [columns],
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

**Key Schema Elements:**
- Lines 14-22: Basic structure with UUID + timestamps
- Lines 76-80: JOIN table pattern (see `semantic_release_sources`)
- Lines 101-105: CHECK constraints for multi-type tables (see `subscriptions`)

---

### 2. Notification Worker: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/worker.go`

**NotifyWorker.Work() Method:**
- **Lines 81-154**: Main entry point. Creates `Notification` struct at lines 132-143
- **Line 132-143**: `Notification` struct construction pattern:
  - Fetches release data (line 82)
  - Checks version filters (lines 88-95)
  - Resolves subscriptions (line 108)
  - Iterates subscriptions (line 113-148)
  - For each: gets channel (line 114), builds Notification (lines 132-143), sends via appropriate sender (line 145)

**Store Interface (NotifyStore):**
- **Lines 19-27**: Defines all required store methods:
  - `GetRelease()`, `GetSource()`, `ListSourceSubscriptions()`, `GetChannel()`, `GetProject()`
  - `GetPreviousRelease()`, `EnqueueAgentRun()`

**Agent Rules Trigger:**
- **Lines 151-198**: `checkAgentRules()` method shows the auto-trigger pattern

---

### 3. Notification Sender Interface: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/sender.go`

**Notification Struct:**
- **Lines 15-24**: Core notification payload:
  ```go
  type Notification struct {
      Title       string
      Body        string
      Version     string
      ProjectName string
      Provider    string
      Repository  string
      ReleaseURL  string
      SourceURL   string
  }
  ```

**Sender Interface:**
- **Lines 27-29**: Simple interface pattern:
  ```go
  type Sender interface {
      Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error
  }
  ```

**Helper Functions:**
- **Lines 61-74**: `ProviderURL()` pattern
- **Lines 77-90**: `ProviderLabel()` pattern
- **Lines 111-148**: `markdownToASCII()` for text formatting

---

### 4. API Routes: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go`

**RegisterRoutes() Function:**
- **Lines 31-124**: Main route registration
- **Pattern for resource CRUD** (lines 42-47, Projects example):
  ```go
  // 1. Create handler instance
  handler := NewXXXHandler(deps.StoreInterface)
  
  // 2. Register routes with chain middleware
  mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/xxx", chain(http.HandlerFunc(handler.List)))
  ```
