package api

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
)

// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
type Dependencies struct {
	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
	AgentStore            AgentStore
	KeyStore              KeyStore
	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
	NoAuth                bool
	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
	HTTPClient            *http.Client
}

// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
	// Public chain: no auth required.
	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)

	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
	chain := publicChain
	if !deps.NoAuth {
		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
	}

	// Projects (CRUD)
	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))

	// Sources (nested under projects)
	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))

	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))

	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))

	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))

	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))

	// Channels (CRUD + test)
	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))

	// Agent
	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))

	// Providers (metadata — static, no store needed)
	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))

	// Discovery (public — no auth, proxies external APIs)
	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))

	// SSE events
	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))

	// Health (public — no auth middleware)
	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
}

1→package routing
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"net/url"
     8→	"regexp"
     9→	"strings"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// Notification is the payload sent through all notification channels.
    15→type Notification struct {
    16→	Title       string `json:"title"`
    17→	Body        string `json:"body"`
    18→	Version     string `json:"version"`
    19→	ProjectName string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    20→	Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    21→	Repository  string `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    22→	ReleaseURL  string `json:"release_url,omitempty"`  // internal Changelogue link
    23→	SourceURL   string `json:"source_url,omitempty"`   // upstream provider link
    24→}
    25→
    26→// Sender is the interface that all notification channel implementations must satisfy.
    27→type Sender interface {
    28→	Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error
    29→}
    30→
    31→// rawBodyFields contains the human-readable fields extracted from raw release JSON.
    32→type rawBodyFields struct {
    33→	Changelog  string
    34→	ReleaseURL string
    35→}
    36→
    37→// parseRawBody extracts known fields from a raw JSON release body.
    38→func parseRawBody(body string) (rawBodyFields, bool) {
    39→	var raw map[string]interface{}
    40→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(body), &raw); err != nil {
    41→		return rawBodyFields{}, false
    42→	}
    43→
    44→	fields := rawBodyFields{
    45→		Changelog:  stringField(raw, "changelog"),
    46→		ReleaseURL: stringField(raw, "release_url"),
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	if fields.Changelog == "" && fields.ReleaseURL == "" {
    50→		return fields, false
    51→	}
    52→	return fields, true
    53→}
    54→
    55→func stringField(m map[string]interface{}, key string) string {
    56→	v, _ := m[key].(string)
    57→	return v
    58→}
    59→
    60→// ProviderURL builds the upstream release page URL for a given provider.
    61→func ProviderURL(provider, repository, version string) string {
    62→	switch strings.ToLower(provider) {
    63→	case "github":
    64→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://github.com/%s/releases/tag/%s", repository, version)
    65→	case "dockerhub":
    66→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/r/%s/tags?name=%s", repository, url.QueryEscape(version))
    67→	case "ecr-public":
    68→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://gallery.ecr.aws/%s", repository)
    69→	case "gitlab":
    70→		return fmt.Sprintf("https://gitlab.com/%s/-/releases/%s", repository, version)
    71→	default:
    72→		return ""
    73→	}
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ProviderLabel returns a human-readable label for a provider.
    77→func ProviderLabel(provider string) string {
    78→	switch strings.ToLower(provider) {
    79→	case "github":
    80→		return "GitHub"
    81→	case "dockerhub":
    82→		return "Docker Hub"
    83→	case "ecr-public":
    84→		return "ECR Public"
    85→	case "gitlab":
    86→		return "GitLab"
    87→	default:
    88→		return provider
    89→	}
    90→}
    91→
    92→// Regex patterns for markdown-to-ASCII conversion.
    93→var (
    94→	// GitHub PR/issue URLs → #NNN ( url )
    95→	reGitHubPR = regexp.MustCompile(`https?://github\.com/([^/]+/[^/]+)/(pull|issues)/(\d+)`)
    96→	// Markdown links [text](url) → text ( url )
    97→	reMdLink = regexp.MustCompile(`\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)`)
    98→	// Headings ## Text → dashed ASCII box
    99→	reHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$`)
   100→	// Bold **text** and __text__
   101→	reBoldStar = regexp.MustCompile(`\*\*(.+?)\*\*`)
   102→	reBoldUnderscore = regexp.MustCompile(`__(.+?)__`)
   103→	// Inline code `text`
   104→	reInlineCode = regexp.MustCompile("`([^`]+)`")
   105→	// HTML tags
   106→	reHTML = regexp.MustCompile(`<[^>]+>`)
   107→	// Images ![alt](url)
   108→	reImage = regexp.MustCompile(`!\[([^\]]*)\]\([^)]+\)`)
   109→)
   110→
   111→// markdownToASCII converts GitHub-flavored Markdown into clean ASCII text
   112→// suitable for display inside Slack/Discord code blocks.
   113→func markdownToASCII(md string) string {
   114→	s := md
   115→
   116→	// Strip images first (before link processing)
   117→	s = reImage.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   118→
   119→	// Convert GitHub PR/issue URLs to #NNN ( url )
   120→	s = reGitHubPR.ReplaceAllString(s, "#$3 ( $0 )")
   121→
   122→	// Convert markdown links [text](url) → text ( url )
   123→	s = reMdLink.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1 ( $2 )")
   124→
   125→	// Convert headings to ASCII art with dashes
   126→	s = reHeading.ReplaceAllStringFunc(s, func(match string) string {
   127→		parts := reHeading.FindStringSubmatch(match)
   128→		title := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
   129→		dashes := strings.Repeat("-", len(title))
   130→		return dashes + "\n" + title + "\n" + dashes
   131→	})
   132→
   133→	// Strip bold markers
   134→	s = reBoldStar.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   135→	s = reBoldUnderscore.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   136→
   137→	// Strip inline code backticks
   138→	s = reInlineCode.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1")
   139→
   140→	// Strip HTML tags
   141→	s = reHTML.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
   142→
   143→	// Collapse 3+ consecutive blank lines into 2
   144→	reBlankLines := regexp.MustCompile(`\n{3,}`)
   145→	s = reBlankLines.ReplaceAllString(s, "\n\n")
   146→
   147→	return strings.TrimSpace(s)
   148→}
   149→

     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     9→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/rivermigrate"
    10→)
    11→
    12→const schema = `
    13→-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
    14→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    15→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    16→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    17→    description TEXT,
    18→    agent_prompt TEXT,
    19→    agent_rules JSONB,
    20→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    21→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
    25→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sources (
    26→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    29→    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    30→    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400,
    31→    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    32→    config JSONB,
    33→    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34→    last_error TEXT,
    35→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    36→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    37→    UNIQUE(provider, repository)
    38→);
    39→
    40→-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
    41→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS context_sources (
    42→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    43→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    45→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    46→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    47→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    48→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    49→);
    50→
    51→-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
    52→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS releases (
    53→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    54→    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    55→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    56→    raw_data JSONB,
    57→    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    58→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    59→    UNIQUE(source_id, version)
    60→);
    61→
    62→-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
    63→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_releases (
    64→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    67→    report JSONB,
    68→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    69→    error TEXT,
    70→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    71→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    72→    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
    73→);
    74→
    75→-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
    76→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_release_sources (
    77→    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    78→    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    79→    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
    80→);
    81→
    82→-- Notification channels (standalone)
    83→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (
    84→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    85→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    86→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    87→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    88→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    89→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    90→);
    91→
    92→-- Subscriptions: two types (source releases and semantic releases)
    93→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    94→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    95→    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    96→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release')),
    97→    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98→    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→    version_filter TEXT,
   100→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   101→    CHECK (
   102→        (type = 'source_release'   AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   103→        (type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   104→    )
   105→);
   106→
   107→-- Agent runs (scoped to project)
   108→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs (
   109→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111→    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   112→    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   113→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   114→    prompt_used TEXT,
   115→    error TEXT,
   116→    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   117→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   118→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   119→);
   120→
   121→ALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);
   122→
   123→-- API authentication keys
   124→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_keys (
   125→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   126→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   127→    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   128→    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,
   129→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   130→    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   131→);
   132→
   133→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on new releases
   134→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_release_created() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   135→BEGIN
   136→    PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   137→    RETURN NEW;
   138→END;
   139→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   140→
   141→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS release_created_trigger ON releases;
   142→CREATE TRIGGER release_created_trigger
   143→    AFTER INSERT ON releases
   144→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_release_created();
   145→
   146→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on semantic release completion
   147→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   148→BEGIN
   149→    IF NEW.status = 'completed' AND (OLD IS NULL OR OLD.status != 'completed') THEN
   150→        PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'semantic_release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   151→    END IF;
   152→    RETURN NEW;
   153→END;
   154→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   155→
   156→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS semantic_release_trigger ON semantic_releases;
   157→CREATE TRIGGER semantic_release_trigger
   158→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON semantic_releases
   159→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release();
   160→`
   161→
   162→// RunMigrations applies River's schema and the application schema. Idempotent — safe to call on every startup.
   163→func RunMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   164→	migrator, err := rivermigrate.New(riverpgxv5.New(pool), nil)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		return fmt.Errorf("create river migrator: %w", err)
   167→	}
   168→	if _, err := migrator.Migrate(ctx, rivermigrate.DirectionUp, nil); err != nil {
   169→		return fmt.Errorf("river migrations: %w", err)
   170→	}
   171→
   172→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, schema); err != nil {
   173→		return fmt.Errorf("app migrations: %w", err)
   174→	}
   175→
   176→	// Migrate existing subscription type values: source→source_release, project→semantic_release.
   177→	// Drop old CHECK constraints first (both the named type check and the unnamed
   178→	// composite check referencing old values), then UPDATE data, then re-add constraints.
   179→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   180→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_type_check;
   181→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_check;
   182→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'source_release' WHERE type = 'source';
   183→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'semantic_release' WHERE type = 'project';
   184→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_type_check CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release'));
   185→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_check CHECK (
   186→			(type = 'source_release'   AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   187→			(type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL)
   188→		);
   189→	`); err != nil {
   190→		return fmt.Errorf("subscription type migration: %w", err)
   191→	}
   192→
   193→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   194→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_include TEXT;
   195→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_exclude TEXT;
   196→	`); err != nil {
   197→		return fmt.Errorf("source version filter migration: %w", err)
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   201→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS exclude_prereleases BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
   202→	`); err != nil {
   203→		return fmt.Errorf("source exclude_prereleases migration: %w", err)
   204→	}
   205→	return nil
   206→}
   207→

1→package routing
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bytes"
     5→	"context"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    11→)
    12→
    13→type webhookConfig struct {
    14→	URL string `json:"url"`
    15→}
    16→
    17→// WebhookSender sends notifications as JSON POST requests to a configured URL.
    18→type WebhookSender struct {
    19→	Client *http.Client
    20→}
    21→
    22→// webhookSemanticPayload is the structured webhook payload for semantic reports.
    23→type webhookSemanticPayload struct {
    24→	Title       string                `json:"title"`
    25→	Version     string                `json:"version"`
    26→	ProjectName string                `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    27→	Provider    string                `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    28→	Repository  string                `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    29→	ReleaseURL  string                `json:"release_url,omitempty"`
    30→	SourceURL   string                `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
    31→	Report      *models.SemanticReport `json:"report"`
    32→}
    33→
    34→func (s *WebhookSender) Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error {
    35→	var cfg webhookConfig
    36→	if err := json.Unmarshal(ch.Config, &cfg); err != nil {
    37→		return fmt.Errorf("parse webhook config: %w", err)
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	// Try to parse body as SemanticReport for structured output.
    41→	var payload any
    42→	var report models.SemanticReport
    43→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(msg.Body), &report); err == nil && report.Subject != "" {
    44→		payload = webhookSemanticPayload{
    45→			Title:       msg.Title,
    46→			Version:     msg.Version,
    47→			ProjectName: msg.ProjectName,
    48→			Provider:    msg.Provider,
    49→			Repository:  msg.Repository,
    50→			ReleaseURL:  msg.ReleaseURL,
    51→			SourceURL:   msg.SourceURL,
    52→			Report:      &report,
    53→		}
    54→	} else {
    55→		payload = msg
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    59→	if err != nil {
    60→		return fmt.Errorf("marshal notification: %w", err)
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, cfg.URL, bytes.NewReader(body))
    64→	if err != nil {
    65→		return fmt.Errorf("create request: %w", err)
    66→	}
    67→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    68→
    69→	client := s.Client
    70→	if client == nil {
    71→		client = http.DefaultClient
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	resp, err := client.Do(req)
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		return fmt.Errorf("send webhook: %w", err)
    77→	}
    78→	defer resp.Body.Close()
    79→
    80→	if resp.StatusCode >= 400 {
    81→		return fmt.Errorf("webhook returned status %d", resp.StatusCode)
    82→	}
    83→	return nil
    84→}
    85→

1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"google.golang.org/genai"
    13→
    14→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    15→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent/llmagent"
    16→	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    17→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin"
    18→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin/loggingplugin"
    19→	"google.golang.org/adk/runner"
    20→	"google.golang.org/adk/session"
    21→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    22→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/geminitool"
    23→
    24→	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    25→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    26→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    27→)
    28→
    29→// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    30→// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    31→// the resulting semantic release.
    32→type OrchestratorStore interface {
    33→	AgentDataStore
    34→	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    35→	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    36→	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    37→	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    38→	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    39→	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    40→	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    41→	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    42→}
    43→
    44→const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    45→
    46→Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    47→
    48→Use the available tools to:
    49→1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    50→2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    51→3. Check binary/image availability.
    52→4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    53→5. Always search the web for supplementary context about this release
    54→   (community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues, migration guides, direct download links).
    55→
    56→CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    57→Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.
    58→
    59→For download_links: ONLY include URLs where the user can actually download the software — direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) and the GitHub/project release page. Do NOT include CVE links, security advisory URLs, blog posts, documentation, or any non-download URLs.
    60→
    61→The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    62→{
    63→  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    64→  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    65→  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    66→  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    67→  "changelog_summary": "summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    68→  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    69→  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15", "curl -L -o geth.tar.gz https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz"],
    70→  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    71→}`
    72→
    73→// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    74→// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    75→// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    76→func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    77→	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    78→	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    79→		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    80→	}
    81→	if version != "" {
    82→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    83→	} else {
    84→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
    85→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    94→	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    95→	if err != nil {
    96→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   100→	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   101→		Name:        "data_agent",
   102→		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   103→		Model:       llmModel,
   104→		Tools:       functionTools,
   105→	})
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   111→	var searchTool tool.Tool
   112→	var searchModel model.LLM
   113→	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   114→	case "openai":
   115→		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   116→		searchTool = *wsConfig
   117→		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   118→		if err != nil {
   119→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   120→		}
   121→	default: // gemini
   122→		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   123→		searchModel = llmModel
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	// Fetch project sources to give the search agent context about where releases come from.
   127→	sources, _, err := store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, project.ID, 1, 100)
   128→	if err != nil {
   129→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list project sources: %w", err)
   130→	}
   131→	var sourceLines []string
   132→	for _, s := range sources {
   133→		sourceLines = append(sourceLines, fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s", s.Provider, s.Repository))
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	searchInstruction := fmt.Sprintf(
   137→		"You are a web search assistant for the project %q. Always include the project name in your search queries to get relevant results. Focus on this specific project — ignore results for unrelated projects that happen to share the same version number.",
   138→		project.Name,
   139→	)
   140→	if len(sourceLines) > 0 {
   141→		searchInstruction += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nThis project's upstream sources are:\n%s\nUse these repository names and URLs to refine your search queries.", strings.Join(sourceLines, "\n"))
   142→	}
   143→
   144→	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   145→		Name:        "search_agent",
   146→		Description: "Search the web for context about a release — community sentiment, security advisories, adoption statistics, known issues, and migration guides. Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights.",
   147→		Instruction: searchInstruction,
   148→		Model:       searchModel,
   149→		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   150→	})
   151→	if err != nil {
   152→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   156→	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   157→		Name:        "release_analyst",
   158→		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   159→		Model:       llmModel,
   160→		Instruction: instruction,
   161→		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   162→			newTracedAgentTool(dataAgent),
   163→			newTracedAgentTool(searchAgent),
   164→		},
   165→	})
   166→}
   167→
   168→// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   169→// release matching the target version.
   170→func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   171→	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   172→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   175→	}
   176→	if len(sources) == 0 {
   177→		return true, nil
   178→	}
   179→
   180→	for _, src := range sources {
   181→		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   182→		if err != nil {
   183→			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   184→		}
   185→		if !has {
   186→			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   187→				"project_id", projectID,
   188→				"source_id", src.ID,
   189→				"version", version,
   190→			)
   191→			return false, nil
   192→		}
   193→	}
   194→	return true, nil
   195→}
   196→
   197→// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   198→// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   199→// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   200→type Orchestrator struct {
   201→	store     OrchestratorStore
   202→	llmConfig LLMConfig
   203→	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   204→}
   205→
   206→// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   207→// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   208→// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   209→func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   210→	switch cfg.Provider {
   211→	case "openai":
   212→		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   213→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   214→		}
   215→	default: // gemini
   216→		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   217→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   218→		}
   219→	}
   220→	return &Orchestrator{
   221→		store:     store,
   222→		llmConfig: cfg,
   223→		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   224→	}, nil
   225→}
   226→
   227→// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   228→//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   229→//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   230→//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   231→//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   232→//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   233→//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   234→//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   235→func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   236→	// Mark as running.
   237→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   238→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   239→	}
   240→
   241→	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   242→	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   243→	if err != nil {
   244→		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   245→		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   246→		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   247→			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   248→				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   249→		}
   250→		return err
   251→	}
   252→
   253→	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   254→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   255→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   256→	}
   257→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   258→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   262→	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   263→
   264→	return nil
   265→}
   266→
   267→// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   268→// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   269→type agentResult struct {
   270→	semanticReleaseID string
   271→	version           string
   272→	reportText        string
   273→	projectName       string
   274→}
   275→
   276→// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   277→// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   278→func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   279→	// Load project.
   280→	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   281→	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   282→	if err != nil {
   283→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   284→	}
   285→	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   286→
   287→	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   288→	version := run.Version
   289→	if version == "" {
   290→		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   291→		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   292→			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   293→		}
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   297→	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   298→		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   299→		"version", version)
   300→	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   301→	if err != nil {
   302→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   303→	}
   304→
   305→	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   306→	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   307→	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   308→		AppName: "changelogue",
   309→		UserID:  "system",
   310→	})
   311→	if err != nil {
   312→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   313→	}
   314→	sess := createResp.Session
   315→
   316→	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   317→	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   318→		AppName:        "changelogue",
   319→		Agent:          agentInstance,
   320→		SessionService: sessionService,
   321→		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   322→			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   323→			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   324→		},
   325→	})
   326→	if err != nil {
   327→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   328→	}
   329→
   330→	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   331→	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   332→	var userPrompt string
   333→	if version != "" {
   334→		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   335→	} else {
   336→		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   337→	}
   338→	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   339→
   340→	var finalText string
   341→	eventCount := 0
   342→	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   343→		if err != nil {
   344→			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   345→				"run_id", run.ID,
   346→				"event_count", eventCount,
   347→				"err", err,
   348→			)
   349→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   350→		}
   351→		eventCount++
   352→		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   353→			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   354→				if part.Text != "" {
   355→					finalText += part.Text
   356→				}
   357→			}
   358→		}
   359→	}
   360→	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   361→		"run_id", run.ID,
   362→		"event_count", eventCount,
   363→		"output_length", len(finalText),
   364→	)
   365→
   366→	if finalText == "" {
   367→		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   368→			"run_id", run.ID,
   369→			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   370→			"event_count", eventCount,
   371→		)
   372→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   373→	}
   374→
   375→	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   376→	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   377→	if err != nil {
   378→		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   379→			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   380→		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   381→		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   382→			Summary: finalText,
   383→		}
   384→	}
   385→
   386→	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   387→	if err != nil {
   388→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   392→	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   393→	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   394→	if err != nil {
   395→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   396→	}
   397→	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   398→	for _, r := range releases {
   399→		if r.Version == version {
   400→			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   405→	srVersion := version
   406→	if srVersion == "" {
   407→		srVersion = "unknown"
   408→		if len(releases) > 0 {
   409→			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   410→		}
   411→	}
   412→
   413→	now := time.Now()
   414→	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   415→		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   416→		Version:     srVersion,
   417→		Report:      reportJSON,
   418→		Status:      "completed",
   419→		CompletedAt: &now,
   420→	}
   421→
   422→	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   423→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   424→	}
   425→
   426→	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   427→		"run_id", run.ID,
   428→		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   429→		"version", sr.Version,
   430→	)
   431→
   432→	return &agentResult{
   433→		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   434→		version:           srVersion,
   435→		reportText:        finalText,
   436→		projectName:       project.Name,
   437→	}, nil
   438→}
   439→
   440→// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   441→// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   442→func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   443→	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   444→	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   445→		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   446→		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   447→		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   448→	}
   449→}
   450→
   451→// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   452→// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   453→// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   454→func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   455→	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   456→	if err != nil {
   457→		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   458→			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   459→		return
   460→	}
   461→	if len(subs) == 0 {
   462→		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   463→			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   464→		return
   465→	}
   466→
   467→	senders := defaultSenders()
   468→
   469→	msg := routing.Notification{
   470→		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   471→		Body:        result.reportText,
   472→		Version:     result.version,
   473→		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   474→	}
   475→
   476→	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   477→	if o.publicURL != "" {
   478→		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   479→	}
   480→
   481→	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   482→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   483→	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   484→		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   485→		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   486→		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   487→	}
   488→
   489→	for _, sub := range subs {
   490→		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   491→		if err != nil {
   492→			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   493→				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   494→			continue
   495→		}
   496→
   497→		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   498→		if !ok {
   499→			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   500→			continue
   501→		}
   502→
   503→		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   504→			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   505→				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   506→		} else {
   507→			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   508→				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   509→		}
   510→	}
   511→}
   512→
   513→// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   514→// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   515→func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   516→	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   517→	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   518→	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   519→		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   520→		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   521→			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   522→		}
   523→		// Remove closing fence.
   524→		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   525→			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   526→		}
   527→		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	var report models.SemanticReport
   531→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   532→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   533→	}
   534→
   535→	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   536→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   537→	}
   538→
   539→	return &report, nil
   540→}

1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  BatchSubscriptionInput,
    12→  BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput,
    13→  NotificationChannel,
    14→  ChannelInput,
    15→  ContextSource,
    16→  ContextSourceInput,
    17→  SemanticRelease,
    18→  AgentRun,
    19→  HealthStatus,
    20→  Stats,
    21→  TrendData,
    22→  DiscoverItem,
    23→} from "./types";
    24→
    25→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    26→
    27→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    28→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    29→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    30→    ...init,
    31→  });
    32→  if (!res.ok) {
    33→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    34→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    35→  }
    36→  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    37→  return res.json();
    38→}
    39→
    40→// --- Projects ---
    41→
    42→export const projects = {
    43→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    44→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    45→  get: (id: string) =>
    46→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    47→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    48→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    49→      method: "POST",

    51→    }),
    52→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    53→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    54→      method: "PUT",
    55→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    56→    }),
    57→  delete: (id: string) =>
    58→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    59→};
    60→
    61→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    62→
    63→export const sources = {
    64→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>

    66→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    67→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    68→      method: "POST",
    69→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    70→    }),
    71→  get: (id: string) =>
    72→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    73→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    74→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    75→      method: "PUT",

    78→  delete: (id: string) =>

    81→    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {

    85→
    86→export const releases = {
    87→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    88→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    89→      `/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    90→    ),
    91→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
    92→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
    93→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    94→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    95→      `/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    96→    ),
    97→  get: (id: string) =>
    98→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
    99→};
   100→
   101→// --- Context Sources ---
   102→
   103→export const contextSources = {
   104→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>

   106→  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>

   121→
   122→// --- Semantic Releases ---
   123→
   124→export const semanticReleases = {

   127→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>

   147→  getRun: (id: string) =>

   149→};
   150→
   151→// --- Subscriptions ---
   152→
   153→export const subscriptions = {
   154→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   155→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>(`/subscriptions?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   156→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`),
   157→  create: (input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   158→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>("/subscriptions", {
   159→      method: "POST",
   160→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   161→    }),
   162→  batchCreate: (input: BatchSubscriptionInput) =>
   163→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   164→      method: "POST",
   165→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   166→    }),
   167→  update: (id: string, input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   168→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, {
   169→      method: "PUT",
   170→      body: JSON.stringify(input),

   172→  delete: (id: string) =>

   212→};

   214→// --- Discovery ---
   215→
   216→export const discover = {
   217→  github: (params?: { q?: string; language?: string }) => {

   219→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);

   221→    const qs = search.toString();
   222→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/github${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);

   228→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/dockerhub${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   229→  },
   230→};

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"os/signal"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12→	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    17→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    18→)
    19→
    20→func main() {
    21→	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    22→	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    23→	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    24→	case "debug":
    25→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    26→	case "warn":
    27→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    28→	case "error":
    29→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    30→	}
    31→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    32→
    33→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    34→	defer stop()
    35→
    36→	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    37→	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    38→	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    39→
    40→	// Database
    41→	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    44→		os.Exit(1)
    45→	}
    46→	defer pool.Close()
    47→
    48→	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    49→		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    50→		os.Exit(1)
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    54→	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    55→	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    56→	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    57→
    58→	// Register River workers
    59→	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    60→	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    61→	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    62→
    63→	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    64→	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    65→	// is restarted.
    66→	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    67→	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    68→	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    69→		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    70→	}
    71→	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    72→		Provider:      llmProvider,
    73→		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    74→		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    75→		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    76→		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    77→	}
    78→	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available — agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    81→	} else {
    82→		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    83→		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    84→		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
    88→	if err != nil {
    89→		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
    90→		os.Exit(1)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
    94→	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
    95→	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
    96→
    97→	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
    98→		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
    99→		os.Exit(1)
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	// Ingestion layer
   103→	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   104→	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   105→
   106→	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   107→	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   108→
   109→	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   110→
   111→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   112→
   113→	// Serve frontend static export if available
   114→	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   115→		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   116→		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	// Register all API v1 routes
   120→	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   121→		DB:                    pool,
   122→		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   123→		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   124→		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   125→		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   126→		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   127→		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   128→		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   129→		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   130→		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   131→		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   132→		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   133→		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   134→		IngestionService:      svc,
   135→		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   136→	})
   137→
   138→	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   139→
   140→	// Start polling in background
   141→	go orch.Run(ctx)
   142→
   143→	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE bridge
   144→	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   145→
   146→	// Start HTTP server
   147→	go func() {
   148→		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   149→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   150→			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   151→		}
   152→	}()
   153→
   154→	<-ctx.Done()
   155→	slog.Info("shutting down")
   156→
   157→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   158→	defer cancel()
   159→	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   160→	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   161→}
   162→
   163→func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   164→	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   165→		return v
   166→	}
   167→	return fallback
   168→}
   169→

    18→type PgStore struct {
    19→	pool  *pgxpool.Pool

    22→
    23→// NewPgStore returns a new PgStore backed by the given connection pool and River client.
    24→// The river client may be nil if agent triggering is not needed.
    25→func NewPgStore(pool *pgxpool.Pool, riverClient *river.Client[pgx.Tx]) *PgStore {
    26→	return &PgStore{pool: pool, river: riverClient}
    27→}
    28→
    29→// SetRiverClient updates the River client on an existing PgStore.
    30→// This is used during bootstrap to inject the client after worker registration.
    31→func (s *PgStore) SetRiverClient(rc *river.Client[pgx.Tx]) {
    32→	s.river = rc
    33→}

    37→func (s *PgStore) ListProjects(ctx context.Context, page, perPage int) ([]models.Project, int, error) {
    38→	var total int
    39→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects`).Scan(&total)
    40→	if err != nil {
    41→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count projects: %w", err)
    42→	}
    43→	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
    44→	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
    45→		`SELECT id, name, COALESCE(description,''), COALESCE(agent_prompt,''),
    46→		        COALESCE(agent_rules,'{}'), created_at, updated_at
    47→		 FROM projects ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, perPage, offset)
    48→	if err != nil {
    49→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list projects: %w", err)
    50→	}
    51→	defer rows.Close()
    52→	var projects []models.Project
    53→	for rows.Next() {
    54→		var p models.Project
    55→		if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.Name, &p.Description, &p.AgentPrompt, &p.AgentRules, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
    56→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("scan project: %w", err)
    57→		}
    58→		projects = append(projects, p)
    59→	}
    60→	return projects, total, nil

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

Let me first read the plan and set up the task list.

    21→
    22→In `internal/db/migrations.go`, add the following to the `schema` constant, after the `semantic_release_trigger` CREATE TRIGGER block (line 159) and before the closing backtick (line 160):
    23→
    24→```sql
    25→-- Release TODOs (acknowledge/resolve tracking)
    26→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS release_todos (
    27→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    28→    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    29→    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    30→    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    31→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    32→    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    33→    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34→    CHECK (
    35→        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
    36→        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
    37→    )
    38→);
    39→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_release_id ON release_todos(release_id) WHERE release_id IS NOT NULL;
    40→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_semantic_release_id ON release_todos(semantic_release_id) WHERE semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL;
    41→```
    42→
    43→Note: Use partial unique indexes instead of UNIQUE constraints since the columns are nullable.
    44→

   106→### Task 3: Store — Add TodosStore to PgStore
   107→
   108→**Files:**
   109→- Create: `internal/api/todos.go` (handler — but write store interface here first)
   110→- Modify: `internal/api/pgstore.go` (add store methods at the end)
   111→
   112→**Step 1: Add store methods to pgstore.go**
   113→
   114→Add the following at the end of `internal/api/pgstore.go`:
   115→
   116→```go
   117→// --- TodosStore ---
   118→
   119→func (s *PgStore) ListTodos(ctx context.Context, status string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Todo, int, error) {
   120→	// Count query
   121→	countQuery := `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM release_todos`
   122→	args := []any{}
   123→	if status != "" {
   124→		countQuery += ` WHERE status = $1`
   125→		args = append(args, status)
   126→	}
   127→	var total int
   128→	if err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, countQuery, args...).Scan(&total); err != nil {
   129→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count todos: %w", err)
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
   133→
   134→	// Build the enriched query with LEFT JOINs to releases and semantic_releases.
   135→	query := `
   136→		SELECT
   137→			t.id, t.release_id, t.semantic_release_id, t.status,
   138→			t.created_at, t.acknowledged_at, t.resolved_at,
   139→			COALESCE(p1.name, p2.name, ''),
   140→			COALESCE(r.version, sr.version, ''),
   141→			COALESCE(src.provider, ''),
   142→			COALESCE(src.repository, ''),
   143→			CASE WHEN t.release_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'release' ELSE 'semantic' END
   144→		FROM release_todos t
   145→		LEFT JOIN releases r ON r.id = t.release_id
   146→		LEFT JOIN sources src ON src.id = r.source_id
   147→		LEFT JOIN projects p1 ON p1.id = src.project_id
   148→		LEFT JOIN semantic_releases sr ON sr.id = t.semantic_release_id
   149→		LEFT JOIN projects p2 ON p2.id = sr.project_id
   150→	`
   151→	queryArgs := []any{}
   152→	argIdx := 1
   153→	if status != "" {
   154→		query += fmt.Sprintf(` WHERE t.status = $%d`, argIdx)
   155→		queryArgs = append(queryArgs, status)
   156→		argIdx++
   157→	}
   158→	query += fmt.Sprintf(` ORDER BY t.created_at DESC LIMIT $%d OFFSET $%d`, argIdx, argIdx+1)
   159→	queryArgs = append(queryArgs, perPage, offset)
   160→
   161→	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx, query, queryArgs...)
   162→	if err != nil {
   163→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list todos: %w", err)
   164→	}
   165→	defer rows.Close()
   166→
   167→	var todos []models.Todo
   168→	for rows.Next() {
   169→		var t models.Todo
   170→		if err := rows.Scan(
   171→			&t.ID, &t.ReleaseID, &t.SemanticReleaseID, &t.Status,
   172→			&t.CreatedAt, &t.AcknowledgedAt, &t.ResolvedAt,
   173→			&t.ProjectName, &t.Version, &t.Provider, &t.Repository, &t.TodoType,
   174→		); err != nil {
   175→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("scan todo: %w", err)
   176→		}
   177→		todos = append(todos, t)
   178→	}
   179→	return todos, total, nil
   180→}
   181→
   182→func (s *PgStore) GetTodo(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Todo, error) {
   183→	var t models.Todo
   184→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `
   185→		SELECT
   186→			t.id, t.release_id, t.semantic_release_id, t.status,
   187→			t.created_at, t.acknowledged_at, t.resolved_at,
   188→			COALESCE(p1.name, p2.name, ''),
   189→			COALESCE(r.version, sr.version, ''),
   190→			COALESCE(src.provider, ''),
   191→			COALESCE(src.repository, ''),
   192→			CASE WHEN t.release_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'release' ELSE 'semantic' END
   193→		FROM release_todos t
   194→		LEFT JOIN releases r ON r.id = t.release_id
   195→		LEFT JOIN sources src ON src.id = r.source_id
   196→		LEFT JOIN projects p1 ON p1.id = src.project_id
   197→		LEFT JOIN semantic_releases sr ON sr.id = t.semantic_release_id
   198→		LEFT JOIN projects p2 ON p2.id = sr.project_id
   199→		WHERE t.id = $1
   200→	`, id).Scan(
   201→		&t.ID, &t.ReleaseID, &t.SemanticReleaseID, &t.Status,
   202→		&t.CreatedAt, &t.AcknowledgedAt, &t.ResolvedAt,
   203→		&t.ProjectName, &t.Version, &t.Provider, &t.Repository, &t.TodoType,
   204→	)
   205→	if err != nil {
   206→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get todo: %w", err)
   207→	}
   208→	return &t, nil
   209→}
   210→
   211→func (s *PgStore) AcknowledgeTodo(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
   212→	tag, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
   213→		`UPDATE release_todos SET status = 'acknowledged', acknowledged_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1`, id)
   214→	if err != nil {
   215→		return fmt.Errorf("acknowledge todo: %w", err)
   216→	}
   217→	if tag.RowsAffected() == 0 {
   218→		return fmt.Errorf("todo not found")
   219→	}
   220→	return nil
   221→}
   222→
   223→func (s *PgStore) ResolveTodo(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
   224→	tag, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
   225→		`UPDATE release_todos SET status = 'resolved', resolved_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1`, id)
   226→	if err != nil {
   227→		return fmt.Errorf("resolve todo: %w", err)
   228→	}
   229→	if tag.RowsAffected() == 0 {
   230→		return fmt.Errorf("todo not found")
   231→	}
   232→	return nil
   233→}
   234→
   235→// CreateReleaseTodo inserts a TODO for a source release. Returns the todo ID.
   236→// Uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency.
   237→func (s *PgStore) CreateReleaseTodo(ctx context.Context, releaseID string) (string, error) {
   238→	var id string
   239→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   240→		`INSERT INTO release_todos (release_id) VALUES ($1)
   241→		 ON CONFLICT (release_id) WHERE release_id IS NOT NULL DO UPDATE SET release_id = EXCLUDED.release_id
   242→		 RETURNING id`, releaseID).Scan(&id)
   243→	if err != nil {
   244→		return "", fmt.Errorf("create release todo: %w", err)
   245→	}
   246→	return id, nil
   247→}
   248→
   249→// CreateSemanticReleaseTodo inserts a TODO for a semantic release. Returns the todo ID.
   250→// Uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency.
   251→func (s *PgStore) CreateSemanticReleaseTodo(ctx context.Context, semanticReleaseID string) (string, error) {
   252→	var id string
   253→	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   254→		`INSERT INTO release_todos (semantic_release_id) VALUES ($1)
   255→		 ON CONFLICT (semantic_release_id) WHERE semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL DO UPDATE SET semantic_release_id = EXCLUDED.semantic_release_id
   256→		 RETURNING id`, semanticReleaseID).Scan(&id)
   257→	if err != nil {
   258→		return "", fmt.Errorf("create semantic release todo: %w", err)
   259→	}
   260→	return id, nil
   261→}
   262→```

   278→### Task 4: API Handler — Todos Endpoints

   280→**Files:**
